Full job description
Join the Future of Commerce with Whatnot!
Whatnot is the largest live shopping platform in North America and Europe to buy, sell, and discover the things you love. Weâ€™re re-defining e-commerce by blending community, shopping, and entertainment into a community just for you. As a remote co-located team, weâ€™re inspired by innovation and anchored in our values. With hubs in the US, UK, Germany, Ireland, Poland, and Australia, weâ€™re building the future of online marketplaces â€“together.

From fashion, beauty, and electronics to collectibles like trading cards, comic books, and even live plants, our live auctions have something for everyone.

And weâ€™re just getting started! As one of the fastest growing marketplaces, weâ€™re looking for bold, forward-thinking problem solvers across all functional areas. Check out the latest Whatnot updates on our news and engineering blogs and join us as we enable anyone to turn their passion into a business, and bring people together through commerce.

Role

We are looking for intellectually curious, highly motivated individuals to join us post grad in 2026. The most important skills youâ€™ll need to have are making sound product decisions and shipping high-quality products fast. We look for engineers with great product instincts who can own whole projects from start to finish.

The engineering team operates with a high level of autonomy so we need to hire people we can trust. Your ability to make good trade-offs and exercise good judgment is essential to us. Some job duties include:

Building new features for users in our live-streams

Implementing growth tactics and strategies to scale our buyer and seller flows

Building systems at scale that help us deliver a high trust marketplace experience

Writing product or system development code

Designing, implementing, and operating our most critical and scalable services

Carefully evaluating the technical tradeoffs of every decision

Supporting features at every phase, from design through implementation and launch

Triage product or system issues and debug/track/resolve by analyzing the sources of issues, network, or service operations and quality.

We offer flexibility to work from home or from one of our global office hubs, and we value in-person time for planning, problem-solving, and connection. Team members in this role must live within commuting distance of our San Francisco, Los Angeles, or Seattle hub.
